joke: I told my suitcase there’d be no vacation this year. Now I’m dealing with emotional baggage.
Why don’t skeletons fight each other? They don’t have the guts.
I only know 25 letters of the alphabet. I don’t know y.
What did one wall say to the other? “I’ll meet you at the corner.”
Why did the tomato blush? Because it saw the salad dressing.
Why was the math book upset? It had too many problems.
What does a cloud wear under its raincoat? Thunderwear.
Why did the belt get arrested? It held up a pair of pants.
Why was the piano so good at keeping secrets? It always stayed low-key.
Why did the photographer get promoted? They always focused on the big picture.
Why did the broom arrive late? It over-swept.
Why did the invisible man turn down the job offer? He couldn’t see himself doing it.
I asked my phone for some space, so it deleted all my contacts.
I entered a procrastination contest, but I haven’t gotten around to competing yet.
I’m reading a book about anti-gravity. It’s impossible to put down.
Why did the duck get a raise? He always put it on the bill.
My boss told me to have a good day, so I went home.
I used to be indecisive. Now I’m not so sure.
I’m great at sleeping—I can do it with my eyes closed.
I organized a hide-and-seek tournament, but good players are hard to find.
My password is “incorrect,” so whenever I forget it, the computer reminds me: “Your password is incorrect.”
I used to hate facial hair, but then it grew on me.
Why are balloons so expensive? Inflation.
Why don’t scientists trust atoms? Because they make up everything.
Why did the mirror get invited to every party? It always reflected well on everyone.
Déjà vu is just your brain saying, “Previously on you…”
I tried to make a joke about time, but it was too soon.
I bought a boomerang that wouldn’t come back. Then I realized—it was just a stick.
I told a joke about silence. It got no response.
I asked my chair for support. It said, “I’ve got your back.”
